Q1_coverage_gaps: All major entry points now covered. Minor gaps: session.Generate direct injection (covered by PH-07/PH-19 chains), UAA authenticator (not a current Harbor feature)
Q2_chain_seeds: PH-25 (Nginx does not strip Harbor-Secret) -> if exploitable: unauthenticated full-trust access to all endpoints
PH-26 (LDAP null bind) -> authentication bypass for LDAP users with empty password acceptance by LDAP server
Q3_fragile_items: PH-16, PH-18, PH-20 remain fragile — no new evidence changes their status
Q4_model_coverage_gaps: UAA auth mode (uaa.go) — not current production feature, low priority
DB auth password hashing algorithm — not yet confirmed
loops.reason: All high-priority entry points covered. New HIGH findings from loop 2: Nginx does not strip Harbor-Secret header, LDAP null bind. Remaining gaps are low priority (UAA) or low severity fragile items. Proceeding to summary.

Q1_coverage_gaps: proxyCacheSecret generator (low priority internal path — no attacker input)
session.Generate race condition with Redis data injection (partially covered by PH-07/PH-19 but direct session.Generate attack path not formally hypothesized)
LDAP null bind attack (ldap.go Bind with empty password) — entry point SearchUser -> Bind sequence not covered
Q2_chain_seeds: PH-01 (Admin DBAuth bypass) chains into PH-06 (no distributed lockout) -> complete admin brute force chain
PH-07 (Redis session token leak) chains into PH-19 (OIDC onboard session trust) -> Redis write -> admin account creation
PH-04 (OIDC admin group injection) chains with OIDC CLI path -> persistent admin access on every CLI request
PH-02 (PKCE bypass) chains with open redirect in onboard URL (PH-09) -> possible CSRF/phishing amplification
Q3_fragile_items: PH-16 (idToken dual parse): fragile finding — requires provider key rotation timing exploitation, LOW severity
PH-18 (auth proxy web vs CLI path): fragile — requires deep TokenReview implementation analysis
PH-20 (providerHelper refresh race): fragile — availability concern, not security vulnerability
Q4_model_coverage_gaps: LDAP authentication path (ldap.go) not covered by either reasoner — specifically null bind bypass and DN injection
Trust chain gap: Nginx header stripping of Harbor-Secret (Gap 1 in attack surface map) not hypothesized as a specific finding
Trust chain gap: v2auth middleware security context check not covered (what happens if security context is nil?)
Q5_impact_multiplication: PH-07 (Redis session leak) validated -> amplifies PH-19 from MEDIUM to HIGH (CROSS-04 chain)
PH-06 (lockout bypass) validated -> amplifies PH-01 from MEDIUM to HIGH (CROSS-01 chain)
If LDAP null bind is confirmed -> would add new HIGH finding for LDAP auth mode
loops.reason: Q1 has LDAP null bind uncovered. Q3 has PH-16/PH-18/PH-20 fragile items that could be re-investigated. Q4 has LDAP path entirely uncovered and Nginx secret header stripping gap not investigated. Loop 2 will focus: (1) LDAP null bind attack in ldap.go, (2) Nginx secret header stripping gap, (3) v2auth nil security context behavior.
